author: y4rr
content: Re-dispatch result: framework tightening visibly worked. Per-crate matrix went from 2 D-defers + 3 wishy-washy options to 7 committed A/B/C decisions with cost estimates. Specific changes: ferrotorch-cubecl D→B (API redesign committed, Step 4 coord block filed); ferrotorch-xpu D→B (cascade committed); ferrotorch-jit B-with-3-options→A (single eager-GPU fallback); ferrotorch-nn B-opt-in→A (write GPU reduce-norm kernel); ferrotorch-distributions C-composite→B-with-Phase-3-kernel-followup (honest about lgamma/digamma always-CPU gap); ferrotorch-distributed C→B (NCCL routing as PyTorch-aligned default, host-roundtrip as documented regression). Open questions: 5→2, both legitimately Rust-specific (logger crate choice tracing-vs-log; sequencing one-PR-vs-two for the B fallback rollout). Subagent explicitly confirmed reading the new §3 defer-clause and Step 3 #12 forbidden pattern at top of output. Recommendation summary names a concrete next action: 'add tracing to workspace deps, ~30 min, item 1 in sequencing'. Sequencing has 9 numbered items with cost estimates and prerequisite arrows. Step 4 coordination has 5 explicit cross-crate items. Framework converged + the discipline tightening verified to change subagent behavior end-to-end.
